title: Design a GenServer's API and state
description: Separate the public client API from the server callbacks and keep the loop fast and crash-safe.
body: Steps: 1. Define the public functions (start_link, and synchronous/asynchronous operations) that wrap GenServer.call/cast so callers never touch internals. 2. Implement init/1 to build a minimal valid state, deferring slow setup to handle_continue. 3. Map each public call to a handle_call (needs a reply) or handle_cast (fire-and-forget), keeping each clause fast. 4. Define the child_spec and place it under a supervisor. 5. Test through the public API, not the callbacks. Rollback point: before adding state fields that change the init contract. Validation: ExUnit drives the public API, concurrent calls stay responsive, and a crash restarts to the init state.
